backstory: Engineer noodling on side projects. Repo is mostly README + a few utility scripts (`summarize.js`, `chunk-pdf.js`, `dedup-rss.js`). No customers yet. Curious about Mur as 'maybe I could publish these as paid APIs'.

repo_signals.stack: node + a handful of utility scripts, no framework

expected_wow: Two-stage. (1) Four-pillar sweep at turn 2: 'What you're building' frames it honestly as a side project ('utility scripts you're polishing'); 'Who's working on it with you' is dropped (solo, no team to honestly describe); 'What we noticed' leads with publish candidates as sub-CTAs (`Try: /mur publish lib/summarize.js`); 'What we can connect to' surfaces gh + OpenAI SDK. (2) Recommend opens post-connect (if user connects) with a `light` propose for publishing lib/summarize.js (one of the few flows that fits this persona — a co-designed publish-watcher that pings them when usage starts). Recommend honestly notes that the digest will be quiet until they ship. The user can describe what they actually want — recommend's co-design surface is the unlock here.
expected_dropoff_risk: Bounces if scan pads pillars that have no signal. The 'Who's working on it with you' pillar should be DROPPED for solo personas with no team, not awkwardly say 'Just you. Connect to expand.' Same principle for recommend: don't pad propose with marquee flows that don't fit ('@mur/uptime-ping' is wrong for a user with no production URL). H16 edge case applies — for this persona, all-co-designed is correct (zero marquee fit) and the explicit note should fire.
wow_score_target: H6 = 3 (sweep at turn 2), H5 ≥ 3 (specific file names), H8 ≥ 3 ('side project' framing honest), H11 = 2 (3 of 4 pillars render — 'Who' dropped honestly), H12/H13/H15 yes, H16 ≥ 2 (all-co-designed with explicit note is the correct mix here)
scoring_notes: Tests honest pillar omission AND honest co-design dominance. A pre-product persona should see THREE pillars (drop 'Who' since solo with no team), NOT four padded ones. Recommend's propose for this persona is mostly co-designed (publish-watcher, OpenAI-cost-monitor) since the marquee catalog doesn't fit a pre-shipping utility-script repo. H16 specifically validates that the all-co-designed mix carries the 'no marquee fits' note rather than padding with @mur/uptime-ping or similar.
